  History of Dragon Ball Z Unlimited:

Although I'd love to take credit for all of the content on this site, that would just be wrong. I didn't write a lot of the stuff you see on this site. I give credit where credit is due and that is just what I'm going to do...er...before I started to work on DBZ Unlimited there was a previous owner who goes by the name of Kemonsa. He had posted on his page that he was looking for someone with HTML experience to take over DBZ Unlimited because school was taking so much of his time (something I can relate to). Of course after reading this, I jumped to the chance.

I already had my own webpage entitled Sailor Moon Unlimited and the two would be a perfect match. To me no other person was worthy of having this page. Apparently Kemonsa agreed and handed his badge and gun over to me! I had seen the show a couple of times, before it began syndication on the Cartoon Network, and like it a lot. Before I actually got into Anime, Dragon Ball Z was airing on the Japanese Cable channel and although I hadn't any idea what the characters were saying (as I don't speak Japanese and there were no english subtitles) every Sunday night at 8 o'clock I watched it religiously until it was canceled from the network. The animation was different than the American Style that I was used and is probably what had attracted me to it.

I don't know as much about DBZ as the average fan but Kemonsa of course knows a lot about the show, as you can see from our extensive library of information found in the "Learn" section of the site. The bios and summaries were excellently written and in depth, a rarity among the DBZ sites I've come across. Although the content was excellent and informing to the reader it was presented poorly as an HTML document. After I fixed a couple of things in the navigation it was easier to access the pages and so I was able to make my very own contribution to the site.

I plan to write my own sections to help this site grow. During the summer of year 2000 I'll be getting a job so that I may scrounge up enough money to get DBZ Unlimited its own domain and get rid of these annoying angelfire pop-ups. If you'd like to contact Kemonsa and pat him on the back for the great work he's done or just ask a question or two, you can e-mail him at kemo69@hotmail.com.

As for other "Dragon Ball Unlimited" sites? Well this is the one, original, and genuine Dragon Ball Z Unlimited. I know for a fact this site was up before any others because I use to surf the DBZ web on a regular basis. This used to be the only one to come up on the search engines.
